data_in.description: Six salmon quant.sf.gz quantification files from tximportData package
tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv file mapping transcript IDs to gene IDs

data_out.description: Gene-level counts matrix (rows: genes, columns: samples)
Gene-level abundance matrix in TPM (rows: genes, columns: samples)
Gene-level length matrix of effective transcript lengths (rows: genes, columns: samples)

domain_knowledge: tximport corrects for potential changes in gene length across samples from differential isoform usage, enabling more accurate gene-level inference.
The tx2gene mapping file is required to associate transcript-level quantification estimates with gene identifiers for aggregation.
The type argument in tximport must be set to 'salmon' to correctly parse salmon quant.sf.gz output format.
The returned list contains three matrices: abundance (estimated TPM), counts (estimated number of reads assigned), and length (effective transcript length per gene per sample).

direct_checks: verify file 'tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv' exists in tximportData package
verify six salmon quant.sf.gz sample files exist in tximportData package
script_runs: tximport function executes without error on the six salmon samples with tx2gene mapping
output_matches_reference: counts matrix dimensions are consistent with gene count (rows) × sample count (columns)
output_matches_reference: abundance matrix dimensions are consistent with gene count (rows) × sample count (columns)
output_matches_reference: length matrix dimensions are consistent with gene count (rows) × sample count (columns)
verify counts matrix contains non-negative numeric values
verify abundance matrix contains non-negative numeric values
verify length matrix contains positive numeric values
expert_review: verify that the three returned matrices (counts, abundance, length) are biologically plausible for RNA-seq transcript aggregation, robust to parameter choices
verify that length-corrected counts appropriately account for differential isoform usage across samples

evidence_snippets.text: [results] Can tximport successfully import salmon transcript-level quantification files and produce gene-level counts, abundance, and length matrices using a pre-constructed tx2gene mapping?: 'The tximport package has a single function for importing transcript-level estimates. The `type` argument is used to specify what software was used for estimation. A simple list with matrices,'

[results] tximport with type='salmon' and a tx2gene mapping produces a list containing gene-level abundance, counts, and length matrices from salmon quant.sf.gz files.: 'library(tximport)
txi <- tximport(files, type="salmon", tx2gene=tx2gene)
names(txi)
head(txi$counts)'
[methods] Six salmon quant.sf.gz quantification files from tximportData package: 'Importing transcript abundance with tximport'
[methods] tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv file mapping transcript IDs to gene IDs: 'pre-built tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv'

[intro] Gene-level counts matrix (rows: genes, columns: samples): 'A simple list with matrices, 'abundance', 'counts', and 'length', is returned, where the transcript level information is summarized to the gene-level'

[intro] Gene-level abundance matrix in TPM (rows: genes, columns: samples): 'A simple list with matrices, 'abundance', 'counts', and 'length', is returned, where the transcript level information is summarized to the gene-level'

[intro] Gene-level length matrix of effective transcript lengths (rows: genes, columns: samples): 'A simple list with matrices, 'abundance', 'counts', and 'length', is returned, where the transcript level information is summarized to the gene-level'
[intro] tximport: 'Imports transcript-level abundance, estimated counts and transcript lengths, and summarizes into matrices for use with downstream statistical analysis packages'
[intro] readr: 'While tximport works without any dependencies, it is significantly faster to read in files using the readr package'
[discussion] No changelog or version history documented for the tximportData package: '_No changelog found._'

cmd: library(tximport); library(readr); tx2gene <- read_csv('{inputs.tx2gene_mapping}'); files <- c({inputs.salmon_quant_files}); names(files) <- sub('\.quant\.sf\.gz$', '', basename(files)); txi <- tximport(files, type='salmon', tx2gene=tx2gene); write.csv(txi$counts, 'gene_counts.csv'); write.csv(txi$abundance, 'gene_abundance.csv'); write.csv(txi$length, 'gene_length.csv')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Gene-level counts matrix (rows: genes, columns: samples)
Gene-level abundance matrix in TPM (rows: genes, columns: samples)
Gene-level length matrix of effective transcript lengths (rows: genes, columns: samples)
finding: tximport with type='salmon' and a tx2gene mapping produces a list containing gene-level abundance, counts, and length matrices from salmon quant.sf.gz files.
inputs: Six salmon quant.sf.gz quantification files from tximportData package
tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv file mapping transcript IDs to gene IDs
methodology_summary: Load the tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv file containing transcript-to-gene ID mappings.
Read six salmon quant.sf.gz files and parse quantification estimates using tximport with type='salmon'.
Aggregate transcript-level estimates to gene-level by summing abundance and counts across all transcripts per gene, weighted by effective length.
Extract and export three gene-level matrices: counts (read assignments), abundance (TPM), and length (effective transcript length per sample).
Validation: confirm that output matrices have correct dimensions (genes × samples), contain non-negative numeric values, and match expected gene IDs from tx2gene mapping.
missing_information: No changelog or version history documented for the tximportData package

task_description: Import transcript-level abundance estimates from six salmon quant.sf.gz samples using tximport with the pre-built tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv mapping, and generate gene-level counts, abundance, and length matrices.
task_objective: Import six salmon quant.sf.gz samples using tximport with tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv and output gene-level counts, abundance, and length matrices.
title: Reproduce tx2gene table construction from Gencode v27 and tximport gene-level count matrix

workflow_description: 1. Load the tx2gene mapping file (tx2gene.gencode.v27.csv) containing transcript-to-gene associations. 2. Read the six salmon quantification files (quant.sf.gz) using tximport with type='salmon' argument. 3. Summarize transcript-level estimates to gene-level by mapping transcripts to genes via tx2gene. 4. Generate and export three matrices: gene-level counts, abundance (TPM), and effective transcript length.
